So what I'm trying to turn this into now, everything so far this morning has been understanding like your core origin story, right? And that's typically the first thing I'm telling someone on a webinar, I'm telling that story, my first big attempt to knock down the big domino. But what happens after that is people have other concerns and other things come up and so you can go and try to like combat every single concern like most people do or you can get really good at the story selling process where you can tell stories that kind of knock all those things down while you're doing it, which is kind of the whole point of the perfect webinars we go through. Now the first thing that I want you guys to understand is that in all of your customers and all of my customers and everybody's customers, this is how, let me step back. So the goal of this, of all our stories is to create belief in somebody, right? And usually though like they have some belief pattern that's already happening, they already believe in it, right? Their belief and we're trying to get them to shift like that vehicle, that belief, that thing you have is not the right vehicle. We need to change your belief to something over here, right? And so you could think about that right this way first like these are the beliefs of our customers, right? They have these beliefs and I call them chains of beliefs, which sometimes beliefs can be positive sometimes they can be negative, right? But for our intents and purposes typically we're looking at like whatever our customers beliefs are aren't right and so we had to help break those chains so we can give them the truth and help free them from whatever it is they can get the results of our vehicle. So everyone's got these beliefs, okay? Now how do we create our beliefs? What happens if you look at it somewhere in your life, you're sitting here, you're going along, everything's fine and then you have some kind of experience, right? So something happened to you, somebody punched you or you tried to do something that didn't work and it failed or whatever that thing might be. There's something happened, you had this experience and then experience led to a story you started telling yourself. Here's the story, there's this book and that story became a belief, right? So if you guys have ever heard Tony Robbins speak or a lot of motivational people they always talk about what's the story you tell yourself? Okay that story is the thing that drives your whole life, your whole future and like you have to change that story if you want to change your life. So I got to realize like what is the story that is holding my customers back and they come back from there and then what is the experience they had? So this is kind of an understanding of the process. When it has an experience, from that experience they create a story, from that story they create a belief pattern, okay? So that process is true in positive things and negative things. So think about just some of the beliefs that you hold true. It could be religious beliefs, it could be fitness beliefs, it could be business beliefs, whatever those things might be and start thinking about like what's the belief you have? So let's say, what's the good one I can say? I have a belief that getting into cryosana three days a week is like the coolest thing in the whole world, right? I believe that, I love it and so much so I bought one and have it in my house and we do that, right? So why do I believe that? Well something happened, I met guys like Anthony had cool videos of me in cryosana, I'm like that looks so cool, I don't know what that is, I want one and then I heard Tony Robbins had one and like I was so excited so I went to San Diego one day and Dave took me, where Dave's at, took me and we actually froze and like I don't know if it did anything but it felt really, really cool. I was like this is awesome, like this is like the coolest thing in the world. So I told myself this story like if I really want to be happy I have to have one of these things in my house so I can do it all the time and it became a belief like if I don't have this I'm going to be miserable and that belief came so real that I went out there and actually ended up getting one, right? So that's like a weird one but it could be for any of your beliefs. I think about your religious beliefs, okay? Everyone here has some type of religious beliefs or nor do religious beliefs, it doesn't really matter it's the same thing. Some experience happened in your life which you created a story from that and that's why you believe what you believe, okay? And so that's kind of the process of how things happen. So that's the first thing, the step we need to understand. So the second step here comes to the false belief patterns. What I'm going to start doing is I want to figure out the reasons why my customers believe things that's holding them back from buying, okay? So the first thing I do is I come out here to the change the false belief and I'm like what is the belief that these guys have that I have to get rid of? So if they're going to buy your product or whatever product, what is the belief that they're holding true that if they believe that there's no way they're going to give you money, okay? So for me a belief could be, oh I'm okay because I already have Infusionsoft. I already have Infusionsoft, I'm good. So I'm going to be like okay, why do they believe that? And say okay, what was the experience they probably went through? So they went through experience, why do they have this, what was the experience they had? And so their experience could be like well I was at a Dan Kennedy event and they said Infusionsoft was the thing, therefore I need to have that, right? So that becomes the second thing here is their experience is that I was at a Dan Kennedy event. And then they said Infusionsoft is the coolest thing so that's why I have it. So from there they built a story like if I want to be successful online I have to have Infusionsoft and this is the story they tell themselves. No if I want to be successful I have to have it, like Dan Kennedy said that's the story I know like I have to have that, okay? So I'm looking at those are the first things I need to know so this is their story and then for me I have to look at that and say okay, this is their story, do I have a story that helps them understand that their story is not true? And if I can find a Tiffany Bridge story that Trump's theirs, we're like dang I was wrong, like that is better, that's different, then they're going to get rid of their story and they're going to take my story as their new story, okay? And that's the magic in how this whole thing works is the third thing we're figuring out like what is your story here? So I'll go to the network marketing industry because this one's the easiest to kind of think through. So network marketing, they have a false belief like this is someone who like had, who went into network marketing one time and tried it and failed, right? So I have a belief that the only way to make money in network marketing is to bug your friends and family members, okay? Okay, well why do they believe that? Well, I met this guy named Brandon, no, just kidding. So I had an experience, I signed up for BLA company and then I went and I got my phone out and I got my contacts and I called my mom and then my dad and then my brother and then my sister and six phone calls my entire family hated me. So they had this horrible experience, right? So what's their story like? That sucks. Network marketing, your friends and family to hate you. So that's where I'm approaching people. So like if I'm trying to sell the network marketing, they're going to be like, dude, no, I've done that before. It's horrible, right? Like it destroyed my relationships and that's the story and it's hard to break those stories. The reason why it's hard to break people's belief patterns because this thing is so real. So what I have to do is I say, okay, what epiphany bridge story do I have that can help that's better than that, that I can give them that'll replace their story? So I say, no, dude, I have the same story. Boom, backstory. I have the same story that you had. I was in the same spot. I understand that. But guess what I found out? I found out there's this weird thing called the internet where you can actually get people to fill out forms and then you just call the people to actually want to make money. And I tell them my whole story. I do the whole epiphany bridge and all of a sudden I tell my story here and in my story Trump said, they're like, huh, I didn't know that was possible. Whole time I thought this was reality, but it's not true. Like this is not true. Like what you told me is the truth. And as soon as they believe that, then they have a new story. All this stuff goes away. And now their story is your story. That's the magic we're talking about here. So if you look at like, why does Russell tell so many stories? It's because I know like my audience, like what false belief patterns I have to break through to get them to buy. I know what belief patterns like when I'm in the room here, if I want you to understand things I have to give it other belief patterns. I'm telling stories constantly because I'm going through, I've done this multiple times in the past where I'm listening to these things out and trying to figure out what are all the beliefs they have and what stories I have to actually strengthen these things. We'll talk about this late at night, probably like midnight or two in the morning, whenever we get done tonight. But when I create my webinars, I'm thinking through that. What are the things that are holding them back? What are the... So like one thing for example I get all the time is like, well this stuff's so good, why don't you just give it to me for free and then pay me from the money you make. How many have you ever gotten that before? So I'm like, crap, that's a good question. Why don't I just give it to you for free and then you give me money back. And so I thought back in my life, what's the story that I have and also I realized, this is the thing, right, they believe that it's so good I should get for free. Why they believe that? Because they bought everyone else's crap and it's scammed and they make them any money. So I had the thing like, everyone's a scam artist. If it was really true, you'd give me for free, right? So what's my story that's better? So for me, some of you guys have heard me say it's on webinars in different places. So when I was wrestling, boom, back story, boom, camera here. So when I was wrestling at BYU, my first year there, I walked in and my head coach was Mark Schultz. I don't even know what Mark Schultz is. You see the movie Foxcatcher? So his brother was murdered by John DuPont and the guy that, how do you say his name? Chainem Taning? I can never say his name. That guy played my coach. And so my coach happens to be the strongest man in earth. My coach was trying to fight Mike Tyson and Mike Tyson was telling. That's how tough my coach is, right? And so anyway, I show a picture of my coach beating the crap out of a guy in a UFC fight. And the strongest man on earth, the Olympic champion, like, like legitimately probably the toughest man that's ever been on this planet. And so he's my coach. So I walk in there, I'm like an awkward little freshman. I think I'm all tough. And I walk in and see this coach. I'm like, holy crap. That's Mark Schultz. Like that's the guy. And Mark looked at me the first time we're wrestling. He's watching me. And then like the next, that night, I'm at home in my dorm with my buddy. And also I hear a knock on the door. I hear the knock, right? And I open it and Coach Schultz is standing there. I'm like, oh my gosh. He's an Olympic champion in my freaking room. Like this is like the coolest thing ever. Like I'm totally freaking out, right? He comes in and he has a VHS tape, which I actually found while we were moving, which is kind of cool. And he had the VHS tape and he hands it to me. I'm like, what's this? He's like, and it says, on the front says, total violence. That's the name he had handwritten on the tape. Hands it to me. He said, this is the highlight tape of all my best matches. He's like, watch this every single day. You become a better wrestler. I'm like, cool. Thanks coach. He's like, go get your wallet. Like, what do you mean? He's like, go get your wallet. I'm like, okay, like my idol Olympic champ could kill me with his bare hands. I'm like, okay, hold on. So I get my wallet. I grab it. I bring it over to handed my coach the wallet. He opens it up, pulls out the money out of it, puts it in his pocket, hands it back and empty wallet. I was like, coach, what are you doing? He's like, I'm teaching you a lesson that'll change your life. If I give this tape for free, you would put it on the shelf. You never would have watched it. But now you've invested in it. So now you're going to watch it and you're going to become better because of that. I was like, okay, coach, I understand. And I did. He left. I was so scared. I should have watched it. I was like, okay, I'm going to get over and over again. And I became a better wrestler. And so the same thing for you. I could give you this crap for free, but guess what's going to happen? You're going to set it up on the shelf and never use it. So I'm going to make you invest because if you invest it, then you're actually going to use it just like my coach did for me. And I care about you guys that much. I'm going to let you invest in this. And all of a sudden that story is like, dude, you're right. I need to invest. I've tried before and I've had success. And all of a sudden I replace their story with mine. And that belief disappears. And now they believe they have to give me money to be successful. Domino falls down. Is that cool? So for you guys, what I want to do right now, because people always ask me, Russell, how do you have so many stories? Part of what I've been doing is for so long, I tell stories over and over and over again. Part of it is one of my first mentors, Dan Kennedy, said you should make an inventory list of all of your stories and learn them. That way you can pop them out whenever you need them for any circumstance, any situation, whenever you have them. Okay? So I want to do, I want to spend some time on this. I want you guys, in fact on the next page here, is I want, in fact in the book we have it sideways. There's an image of this sideways that has all the lines. I want you to think through this. And outside of this event you should spend a lot more time doing this. But for right now I want you guys to think of at least 10 things. Okay? What are 10 false beliefs that your people have right now? And so this goes out as fast as you can kind of go through them. Okay? They believe that they have to do this. They believe that this, like whatever false beliefs you know that your customers have, they're keeping them from giving you money and joining your cult. Sure? What are all those things, right? So then after that, I think they're okay. Well, what was the experience they probably had that gave them that thought? So that's Robert Kiyosaki's story, but I tell it when I need it so I can pull it out. So it doesn't always have to be your story, but what are the stories you have that can counteract each of these? Because these are going to be the things that are holding up that domino. Like, I believe that, but what about this? What about this? And they have all these what abouts that are holding this domino up. So if I can tell stories of my presentation, it will knock these things down and eventually the whole domino has to fall. Boom. Cool. All right. So for all of you guys as you're creating your presentations and doing anything it's like always thinking through this. Every time you see concern when you're in a webinar you see the chats coming in or you do a comment and someone's commenting. Every time that comes instead of being like oh crap that's like sometimes I think especially as product creators like create something and someone gives us a negative feedback and we're like oh and like you want to delete that post or whatever. It was better as coming back and like okay that's if they're saying that means there's probably 20 other people that also believe that like what do I have? What story do I have that I can go and share that with them that also breaks that false belief and then if you do that like in your group whatever then also everyone else has that sympathy too but it also gives you like knowing that as you're doing your other things. We'll talk about the webinar model probably before, then after anyway but like part of the reason why I did my webinar live like 70, 75 times was because I was watching this like what were all the false beliefs people were giving me in the comment section? What are their excuses why they're not buying? What are the reasons why they're refunding afterwards? Like when I understood those things I can come back and I can address them throughout the presentation and that way when it gets to the end the domino falls down and they have to be part of it. There's no more excuses. So that's the key. All right so the next piece of this is now that we have these and if you guys again spend more time go home and keep doing this this is like you should have a notebook which is like stories after story after story after story. The first time I ever heard this was Dan Kennedy had a course that kind of went through this and he said that every single objection you ever get you need to find a story that matches it like every single one is like it's the lifetime of like building up this inventory of stories and after you have them like it just becomes so easy. And so most people are like just hoping that in the moment when a rejection or a thing comes up your brain will just pop it out but it doesn't work that way. The way it pops out of your mind is because you've written it down you know you've practiced you said that story and then it comes out all the time and you're like oh cool I like this situation like half the stuff I felt like I don't really have like super organized plans I have like a direction I'm going because I'm talking like I feel like from you guys stuff and then like stories that start popping out but it's only because I've told those stories a hundred times before like okay boom this will help them understand that and this will help them understand this and they're probably getting stuck here so I need to share this story that will help them understand that. Right so the more you're doing it the more they start popping up and it's kind of they come to you when you need it. This is the coolest thing in the world but only if you've developed those it's like talents right if you're not developing your talents like when it's your shot to like use it you're like an idiot but if you're developing it and every time you're doing Facebook Live you're just telling a story like you're doing a blog post you're telling a story you're doing a podcast you're telling like consistency of doing this over and over and over again. I'm just finished I think I'm episode 306 or 708 in my podcast it's like 300 times I've had a chance to tell my stories over and over and over again Facebook Live like all these things I'm just always doing it so that when I need it boom it's there and I don't have to think like how does the story go like it just it pops in your head when you need it it's the coolest thing in the world.
